turns.content: Hey, I've been trying to port an old C project that uses tar files over here, but I'm still fuzzy on how the crate handles streaming extraction without loading everything into memory. I read somewhere that you can use the Read trait with some wrapper, but I don't quite grasp how to actually implement this in my code.

You're mixing up a couple of things. The crate itself doesn't let you just read files as streams directly; it wraps the file handle so you can extract entries one by one using an iterator over the archive's files. You need to open your tar file, then iterate through its contents and process each entry individually without buffering the whole thing.
Wait, I thought opening a file gave me a File object that already implements Read? So why can't I just pipe it straight into something like BufReader or similar to get streaming behavior?

That's a common misunderstanding. While File does implement Read, the crate wraps that file in an Archive structure specifically so you can iterate over its entries without loading them all into memory at once. You still need to handle the file handle separately for streaming reads if your logic requires it, but the archive itself provides the iterator pattern for extracting files.
